title: I Made $7,245 After Just 20 Days Of Posting YouTube Shorts

transcript.text: This is a YouTube channel I started in February. This was the first video I posted, video two, and video seven, and this is it now. Over 100 videos that got more than 10 million reach. And in today's video, I'll walk you through four unique scriptwriting frameworks that I used to blow up this channel from zero to over 150,000 followers in just 11 videos. Let's start with the first video I ever posted. So, as you can see on the channel, the first ever video was posted January 27. Uh and uh it currently sits at 186,000 views, but it wasn't always like that. So, at the beginning when I posted this, uh it got stuck for about 2 weeks at 12,000 views. So, I pretty much got view jailed like many of faceless YouTube creators, um especially shorts creators. Uh but uh again, once the channel started to uh pick back up, uh the the video kind of exploded. Obviously, 186k uh nothing really. And the way I actually created this video was by using the content upgrade strategy. So, this is one of the fourth strategies that I'm going to show you, and every next strategy is better than the previous one. But, we start with content upgrade. So, the strategy that I call content upgrade is actually very simple. The goal of it is to take a video that blew up in a lower category of content and upgrade its quality by animating it or simply changing the style of content. And uh my favorite, because I run out of war, my favorite example is to always take commentary videos in a war niche that blew up and animating them showing even more details. So, here's what I did. Uh this was the video, and uh uh the way I came up with the idea was actually by watching a very popular meme at the time uh where the guy basically uh shows a video from a from an army where the recommendation that they give to soldiers is uh to buy like a pack of tampons and whenever they get shot just put a tampon inside the wound and it will like absorb the blood and everything. So, I saw this and I was like such a viral angle. There's so many things that I like about the idea and it's it's definitely going viral, right? So, the next thing that I did was I needed to find a format and that's when I saw this video from Simply Simply How, I think. Yeah, Simply How. Where basically the narration goes, if you get stabbed, don't panic cuz this thing can save your life. And this was the exact hook I needed for my video. So, I took this viral like storytelling framework and I took the idea that I liked and I changed being stabbed to basically being shot. So, this is the video that I got. If you get shot, don't panic cuz this can save your life. And then basically I highlighted the key moments of the script that I literally replicated after Simply How. So, as you can see, I replicated the first step framework. Then obviously like there's a some kind kind of a recommendation like why you should be doing something like this. And I also implemented I didn't stop at only tampons. I also added sanitary pads that you should put at the top because like I couldn't quite believe that these are like real recommendations that are given to the soldiers nowadays. And this was super viral for multiple reasons. First of all, when you see a hook like this, you kind of like it's it's very controversial. And the tip itself is crazy controversial. So, combining all of that made me think that the video that I just came up with is absolutely amazing. And I mean it was kind of like it's still one of my favorite videos on the channel. However, as I said, it got stuck at 12K for about 2 weeks and at the time I felt like the video was a failure. It did get by the way about 300,000 views on TikTok. So, it was another sign that I was onto something. But, that was just the first video. Now, when this video didn't blow up, my initial thinking was, "Okay, cool. Maybe I tried something that was too far from what the audience really wants. Maybe I should stick to the reference." And the reference for this channel for me was obviously Zach the Films. So, if you don't know Zach the Films, he's posting those 3D animated videos pretty much similar to mine, but he's doing it in many different niches except instead of just one. And obviously, he gets way more views. So, the second strategy that I decided to use is >> Hey, real quick. You'll find a link in the description. Don't wait. I'll see you there. >> Basically, direct stealing because I thought if my original idea didn't work, maybe I should do something that already worked for Zach the Films and I should just directly steal his video. To me, back then, like if someone posting the second ever video on the channel, it sounded like a good idea, but I didn't know that direct stealing is probably one of the worst strategies out there because basically those videos deliver zero to none value to the audience and the YouTube algorithm quickly realizes that there was a very very similar video on the platform and the audience who watched it already got all of the value that they were looking for and there's no reason for them to watch your video.
